marketState: neutral, cross-currents: bearish storage/trend vs bullish weather demand and a crowded-short COT
summary: Price sits almost exactly at modeled fair value ($2.916 vs $2.923 fair, spread -$0.007) with the fair-value curve itself sloping up toward $3.06 by +21 days, while seasonality shows price -7.5pp below the typical year but with 'Low' reliability and a bearish-tilted 38% odds of higher prices by week 4. Near-term and 8-16 day weather demand are both running ~18-19% above the 5-year norm near the top of their historical range, a genuinely bullish cooling-demand signal for mid-July. That is offset by a bearish +22 Bcf storage surprise (build bigger than forecast, +180 Bcf surplus vs 5y) and daily/weekly technicals still in a bearish trend (RSI high-30s, 50<200 EMA), even as 5m/15m intraday technicals have turned bullish. Managed money is net short at only the 8th percentile of the last 5 years - an unusually crowded short that raises squeeze risk against chasing the bearish trend. FuturesCurve data was unavailable (null) in this snapshot, removing a useful contango/backwardation check.

longTerm.rationale: Primary signals conflict: storage surplus (+180 Bcf vs 5y, bearish surprise) and bearish daily/weekly trend argue for caution on longs, while above-normal weather demand, a rising fair-value curve ($2.92 now to $3.06 by +21D), and an 8th-percentile crowded managed-money short argue against chasing a short here. Seasonality itself is weak evidence (reliability 'Low', 59pp spread) so it isn't the tie-breaker either way.
longTerm.trigger: Wait for one side to confirm: either a daily-trend flip to bullish (price reclaims and holds above ~$3.00 with the 1d/1w trend turning neutral/bullish, validating the weather/COT-squeeze case) to go LONG, or a breakdown through ~$2.75-2.80 alongside another bearish storage surprise (validating the surplus/downtrend case) to go SHORT.

shortTerm.riskReward: ~1:0.7 to TP1, ~1:1.5 to TP2 (take partial at TP1)
shortTerm.sizing: 1R approx 1% account risk; trim ~50% at TP1, trail rest to TP2
shortTerm.rationale: Front price trades right at fair value with 1-7D and 8-16D weather demand both ~18-19% above 5y norms near the top of their range, a near-term bullish cooling-demand tailwind, and 5m/15m technicals have flipped bullish even though 4h/1d/1w remain bearish. This is a tactical, low-conviction bounce riding the weather/fair-value drift toward the plus7D/plus14D fair marks (~$2.95-$3.00), not a trend-following call - the larger daily/weekly downtrend is the main risk.
shortTerm.invalidation: A daily close back below $2.80 breaking the recent range, or the 8-16D weather demand outlook rolling back toward the 5y norm, would invalidate the bounce thesis.
